topic.what: Re-evaluates GCN, GraphSAGE, GAT, and EvolveGCN on Elliptic Bitcoin Dataset under strict inductive protocol with 10-seed evaluation
topic.how: Strict inductive protocol (train on time-step≤34 relabeled subgraph, evaluate on full graph); paired transductive-vs-inductive comparison with 10 matched seeds; per-timestep F1 reporting; graph-structure ablation with shuffled/removed edges
topic.why_matters: Prior published GNN numbers (F1=0.70-0.77) on Elliptic are artefacts of transductive evaluation that exposes test-period adjacency to encoders during training. This work demonstrates that protocol choice explains more variance than architecture choice.

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score on illicit class for Random Forest on raw 165 features under strict inductive protocol
characteristics.vs_baseline: Best model in study; beats every GNN
characteristics.context: 3-layer MLP=0.549, GraphSAGE=0.689, GCN=0.503, GAT=0.610 all below RF under strict inductive

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score for GraphSAGE under strict inductive protocol
characteristics.vs_baseline: Strongest graph encoder under strict inductive
characteristics.context: Falls 13.2 F1 points below raw-feature RF

characteristics.what_itasures: Paired F1 gap for GraphSAGE trained transductively vs inductively with identical architecture, optimizer, loss, seed
characteristics.evidence: Paired t=49.97, p=2.6×10^-12, Cohen's d=15.8, 10 seed-matched runs
characteristics.context: Entirely explained by training-time exposure to test-period adjacency

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 delta from randomly shuffling transaction edges vs using real topology under inductive GraphSAGE
characteristics.vs_baseline: Real edges underperform random by 8.9 F1 points
characteristics.evidence: 10-seed ablation; removing edges entirely still beats real (0.316 vs 0.290)
characteristics.context: Real topology is actively harmful under sparse, prior-shifted conditions

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 lift from GNN over matched-capacity MLP in concatenation hybrid with raw features
characteristics.vs_baseline: Statistically reliable but dwarfed by 0.124 gap to raw features alone
characteristics.evidence: Welch p=0.015, Cohen's d=+1.20; raw RF=0.823 vs hybrid=0.699
characteristics.context: GNN embeddings are a net negative when concatenated to raw features; concatenation dilutes information-per-split budget

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ratio of fraud rate between training period and worst test period
characteristics.evidence: Table 3; 39× reduction from training to minimum test fraud rate
characteristics.context: Prior probability shift causes F1 collapse after step 42; MMD between test steps and training is nearly flat (0.20-0.31), confirming feature distribution is stable

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 change after temperature scaling for GNN models during temporal collapse
characteristics.vs_baseline: Temperature scaling cannot fix threshold-shift problems
characteristics.evidence: Section 8.3; monotone scaling preserves rank, cannot move decision threshold to new Bayes-optimal location
characteristics.context: 39× prior drop is a threshold-shift problem, not a probability-calibration one

good_for: evaluating GNNs on temporally-split transaction graphs
scoping whether graph structure adds signal over feature-only baselines
benchmarking fraud detection under distribution shift
reproducing strict-inductive protocol for future Elliptic comparisons
not_for: Production transductive systems where test-period adjacency is a legitimate input
Datasets with dense homophilous community structure
Graphs with rich edge attributes or typed edges
requires: Access to Elliptic Bitcoin Dataset (publicly available on Kaggle)
Strict exclusion of test-period vectors from training message passing and batch statistics
Per-timestep F1 reporting to expose temporal collapse
